title: Common Skin Problems and Home Remedies
content:
Common Skin Problems and Home Remedies

1. Acne (Pimples & Breakouts)

Causes:

  • • Hormonal imbalance
  • • Oily skin
  • • Clogged pores
  • • Poor hygiene

Home Remedies:

  • • Aloe Vera: Apply fresh aloe vera gel to reduce inflammation and redness.
  • • Tea Tree Oil: Mix a few drops with water and apply to pimples to fight bacteria.
  • • Honey + Cinnamon Mask: Use this natural antimicrobial combo once a week.

🔍 Tip: Avoid touching your face frequently and wash pillow covers regularly.

2. Dry Skin (Xerosis)

Causes:

  • • Weather changes (especially winter)
  • • Hot showers
  • • Harsh soaps or detergents

Home Remedies:

  • • Coconut Oil: Apply after bathing to lock in moisture.
  • • Oatmeal Bath: Soothes irritated and itchy dry skin.
  • • Milk & Honey Pack: Helps moisturize and soften the skin naturally.

🔍 Tip: Use lukewarm water instead of hot water for bathing.

3. Fungal Infections (Ringworm, Athlete's Foot)

Causes:

  • • Humid environment
  • • Sweat retention
  • • Wearing tight or unclean clothes

Home Remedies:

  • • Neem Paste: Natural antifungal properties make it ideal for treating infections.
  • • Apple Cider Vinegar: Dilute and apply to affected areas.
  • • Tea Tree Oil: Works well for fungal infections like athlete's foot.

🔍 Tip: Keep the skin dry, wear loose cotton clothes, and avoid sharing towels.

4. Pigmentation & Dark Spots

Causes:

  • • Sun exposure
  • • Hormonal changes
  • • Post-acne scars

Home Remedies:

  • • Lemon Juice + Honey: Lightens pigmentation naturally (apply only at night).
  • • Potato Juice: Reduces dark circles and spots.
  • • Aloe Vera + Vitamin E: Nourishes and brightens dull skin.

🔍 Tip: Always wear sunscreen, even indoors.

5. Dandruff & Hair Fall

Causes:

  • • Stress
  • • Fungal scalp infections
  • • Poor nutrition

Home Remedies:

  • • Fenugreek (Methi) Seeds: Soak overnight, grind, and apply to scalp.
  • • Onion Juice: Promotes hair growth and reduces scalp infections.
  • • Amla Oil: Strengthens roots and prevents premature graying.

🔍 Tip: Massage your scalp weekly with warm oil and avoid harsh shampoos.

When to See a Dermatologist?

While home remedies can help with mild symptoms, consult a skin specialist if:

  • • The condition worsens or doesn't improve in 1–2 weeks
  • • You notice pus, bleeding, or severe inflammation
  • • Over-the-counter products are not effective
  • • You experience persistent itching, dryness, or hair loss

Final Thoughts

Skin problems are common, but with the right care and natural ingredients, many issues can be handled at home. However, don't hesitate to consult a qualified dermatologist for professional treatment and long-term relief.

title: Laser Treatment for Acne Scars: What to Expect
content:
Laser Treatment for Acne Scars: What to Expect

Acne is a common skin condition, but what often lingers long after the pimples have healed are the scars — a constant reminder of past breakouts. Fortunately, dermatology has come a long way, and one of the most effective treatments available today is laser therapy for acne scars.

In this article, I'll walk you through everything you need to know — from how laser treatment works to what you can expect before, during, and after the procedure.

What Is Laser Treatment for Acne Scars?

Laser treatment uses focused light energy to target and break down scar tissue, while simultaneously stimulating the growth of new, healthy skin. This process helps improve skin texture, reduce pigmentation, and make scars less noticeable.

There are different types of lasers used in dermatology, and the choice depends on the type of acne scars and your skin tone.

Types of Lasers Used

Available Lasers:

  • • Fractional CO2 Laser: Best for deep, pitted scars (boxcar and ice-pick scars). It removes tiny columns of skin to stimulate collagen remodeling.
  • • Erbium YAG Laser: Ideal for more superficial scars. It offers faster healing time and is gentler on the skin.
  • • Pulsed Dye Laser (PDL): Useful for red or inflamed scars. It reduces redness and helps in skin resurfacing.

Who Is a Good Candidate?

Ideal Candidates:

  • • People with moderate to severe acne scars
  • • Individuals with realistic expectations
  • • Those who are in good general health and not currently experiencing active acne
  • • Patients with darker skin tones may require special consideration to avoid post-treatment pigmentation changes.

What to Expect: Step-by-Step

Treatment Process:

  • • 🔹 1. Consultation: Every treatment begins with a thorough consultation. I assess the type and depth of scars, your skin type, and discuss your goals. Based on that, we choose the most appropriate laser.
  • • 🔹 2. Pre-Treatment Preparation: You may be advised to avoid sun exposure for 2 weeks, stop using retinoids or exfoliants, and take antiviral medication (if prone to cold sores).
  • • 🔹 3. The Procedure: A numbing cream is applied to minimize discomfort. The laser is passed over the targeted areas in controlled pulses. The session takes about 30–60 minutes, depending on the area.
  • • 🔹 4. Post-Treatment Recovery: Mild redness, swelling, or a sunburn-like sensation is common for a few days. You may experience light peeling as new skin regenerates. Most patients can resume normal activities in 3–5 days.

Laser Treatment In Ajmer

How Many Sessions Are Needed?

Most patients need 3 to 6 sessions spaced 4 to 6 weeks apart for optimal results. Improvements continue for months as collagen builds up under the skin.

Possible Side Effects

Potential Side Effects:

  • • Redness or swelling
  • • Mild itching or discomfort
  • • Post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation (more common in darker skin tones)

Proper pre- and post-care significantly reduces these risks.

Results You Can Expect

Expected Outcomes:

  • • Smoother skin texture
  • • Faded scars
  • • Even skin tone
  • • Boost in confidence!

Results vary by individual, but most patients report visible improvement after just a few sessions.

Final Thoughts by Dr. Anju Mehra

Laser treatment for acne scars can be truly transformative — not just for your skin, but for your confidence. However, it's important to have realistic expectations and work with a dermatologist who understands your skin's unique needs.

title: Daily Skin Care Routine for Oily, Dry, and Sensitive Skin Types
content:
Daily Skin Care Routine for Oily, Dry, and Sensitive Skin Types

Taking care of your skin daily is essential for maintaining a healthy, glowing complexion. However, the perfect skin care routine can differ depending on your skin type. Here's a simple, dermatologist-approved guide for oily, dry, and sensitive skin.

Skin Care Routine for Oily Skin

Oily skin tends to produce excess sebum, leading to clogged pores and breakouts.

Steps:

  • • Cleanser: Use a gentle, foaming cleanser twice daily to remove oil without stripping skin.
  • • Toner: Apply an alcohol-free toner with salicylic acid to control oil production.
  • • Moisturizer: Choose a lightweight, oil-free moisturizer to hydrate without clogging pores.
  • • Sunscreen: Always use a non-comedogenic, matte-finish sunscreen.

Pro Tip:

Avoid overwashing your face, which can cause your skin to produce even more oil!

Skin Care Routine for Dry Skin

Dry skin feels tight, flaky, and sometimes itchy.

Steps:

  • • Cleanser: Opt for a cream-based, hydrating cleanser to protect your natural skin barrier.
  • • Toner: Use a hydrating toner with ingredients like glycerin or hyaluronic acid.
  • • Moisturizer: Choose a rich, nourishing moisturizer — look for ceramides and shea butter.
  • • Sunscreen: Use a moisturizing sunscreen with SPF 30 or higher.

Pro Tip:

Apply moisturizer while your skin is still slightly damp to lock in moisture.

Skin Care Routine for Sensitive Skin

Sensitive skin reacts easily to new products and environmental changes.

Steps:

  • • Cleanser: Use a fragrance-free, hypoallergenic cleanser.
  • • Toner: Skip harsh toners; instead, apply a soothing mist or calming toner with aloe vera.
  • • Moisturizer: Stick to simple, fragrance-free moisturizers formulated for sensitive skin.
  • • Sunscreen: Use mineral-based sunscreens with zinc oxide or titanium dioxide.

Pro Tip:

Always patch-test new products before full application!

title: Skin Problems, Causes, Signs, and Treatment Options
content:
Skin Problems, Causes, Signs, and Treatment Options

Our skin is the body's largest organ, acting as a protective barrier against external elements. However, it is also prone to various conditions that can affect its appearance, texture, and overall health. In this guide, we will explore common skin conditions, their causes, symptoms, and available treatment options.

Common Skin Conditions and Their Causes

1. Acne

  • • Cause: Hormonal changes, clogged pores, excess oil production, and bacteria.
  • • Symptoms: Pimples, blackheads, whiteheads, and sometimes cystic breakouts.
  • • Treatment: Over-the-counter creams, prescription medications, proper skincare routine, and professional treatments like chemical peels.

2. Eczema (Atopic Dermatitis)

  • • Cause: Genetic factors, allergens, environmental triggers, and immune system dysfunction.
  • • Symptoms: Dry, itchy, red, and inflamed skin, often seen in patches.
  • • Treatment: Moisturizers, anti-inflammatory creams, antihistamines, and avoiding triggers.

3. Psoriasis

  • • Cause: Autoimmune response causing rapid skin cell turnover.
  • • Symptoms: Red, scaly patches that may crack and bleed, commonly found on elbows, knees, and scalp.
  • • Treatment: Topical corticosteroids, phototherapy, oral medications, and lifestyle changes.

4. Rosacea

  • • Cause: Unknown, but linked to genetic and environmental factors.
  • • Symptoms: Facial redness, visible blood vessels, and acne-like breakouts.
  • • Treatment: Prescription creams, laser therapy, and avoiding triggers like spicy foods and alcohol.

5. Fungal Infections (Ringworm, Athlete's Foot, Candidiasis)

  • • Cause: Fungal overgrowth due to warm, moist environments or a weakened immune system.
  • • Symptoms: Itchy, scaly, red patches that may form ring-like shapes.
  • • Treatment: Antifungal creams, oral medications, and maintaining proper hygiene.

6. Skin Cancer (Melanoma, Basal Cell Carcinoma, Squamous Cell Carcinoma)

  • • Cause: UV radiation exposure, genetics, and lifestyle factors.
  • • Symptoms: Unusual moles, skin lesions, or growths that change in size, shape, or color.
  • • Treatment: Surgical removal, radiation therapy, chemotherapy, and targeted therapies.

7. Contact Dermatitis

  • • Cause: Allergic reaction or irritation from skincare products, chemicals, or fabrics.
  • • Symptoms: Red, itchy, inflamed skin, sometimes with blisters.
  • • Treatment: Identifying and avoiding the irritant, topical creams, and antihistamines.

8. Hives (Urticaria)

  • • Cause: Allergic reactions, infections, stress, or unknown triggers.
  • • Symptoms: Red, raised, itchy welts that appear suddenly.
  • • Treatment: Antihistamines, avoiding triggers, and in severe cases, corticosteroids.

9. Vitiligo

  • • Cause: Autoimmune condition leading to loss of skin pigment.
  • • Symptoms: White patches on the skin due to lack of melanin production.
  • • Treatment: Topical creams, light therapy, and in some cases, skin grafting.

10. Cold Sores (Herpes Simplex Virus – HSV)

  • • Cause: Viral infection caused by HSV-1.
  • • Symptoms: Fluid-filled blisters around the mouth or lips, often triggered by stress or illness.
  • • Treatment: Antiviral medications, topical creams, and proper hygiene.

Prevention and General Skincare Tips

  • • Maintain hygiene: Regularly wash your face and body with mild cleansers.
  • • Use sunscreen: Protect your skin from harmful UV rays by using SPF 30 or higher.
  • • Moisturize daily: Keep your skin hydrated to prevent dryness and irritation.
  • • Avoid harsh chemicals: Choose gentle skincare products free from irritants.
  • • Stay hydrated and eat well: A balanced diet rich in vitamins and minerals promotes healthy skin.
  • • Consult a dermatologist: Seek professional help if a skin condition persists or worsens.

title: Hair Loss: Common Causes by Dr. Anju Mehra, Best Dermatologist in Ajmer
content:
Hair Loss: Common Causes by Dr. Anju Mehra, Best Dermatologist in Ajmer

Hair loss is a common problem affecting people of all ages. It is not just a cosmetic issue but can also be a sign of underlying health or lifestyle concerns. Dr. Anju Mehra, a renowned dermatologist in Ajmer, emphasizes the importance of early diagnosis and proper treatment to prevent excessive hair fall.

In this article, we explore the common causes of hair loss and how to address them, with expert insights from Dr. Anju Mehra.

1. Genetic Hair Loss (Androgenetic Alopecia)

One of the leading causes of hair loss is genetics. If you have a family history of baldness, you may also experience hair thinning and receding hairlines. This condition, known as Male Pattern Baldness or Female Pattern Baldness, occurs due to hormonal changes and hereditary factors.

🔹 Solution: Early intervention with Minoxidil or Finasteride, FDA-approved treatments, can slow down hair loss and promote regrowth.

2. Stress and Anxiety

Excessive stress can trigger a condition called Telogen Effluvium, where hair follicles prematurely enter the resting phase, leading to excessive hair fall. Emotional and physical stress, such as work pressure, exams, or personal issues, can contribute to hair thinning.

🔹 Solution: Managing stress through meditation, yoga, and relaxation techniques can help control hair loss.

3. Poor Diet and Nutritional Deficiencies

A lack of essential nutrients like protein, iron, biotin, vitamin D, and zinc can weaken hair follicles, making them prone to breakage and hair loss. Unhealthy eating habits and excessive junk food consumption can further damage hair health.

🔹 Solution: Maintain a balanced diet rich in green leafy vegetables, eggs, nuts, fish, dairy products, and fresh fruits to nourish your hair.

4. Hormonal Imbalance

Hormonal disorders such as thyroid issues, PCOS (Polycystic Ovary Syndrome), menopause, and pregnancy-related hormonal changes can cause excessive hair loss. Women experiencing hormonal fluctuations may notice hair thinning or patchy baldness.

🔹 Solution: If you have irregular periods, unexplained weight gain, or other symptoms of hormonal imbalance, consult a dermatologist or endocrinologist for proper treatment.

5. Overuse of Chemical Products & Heat Styling

Frequent use of harsh shampoos, chemical treatments (rebonding, straightening, perming), and hair dyes can weaken the hair shaft. Additionally, excessive use of heat styling tools like straighteners and curling irons can cause breakage and hair thinning.

🔹 Solution: Switch to sulfate-free, mild shampoos and limit the use of heat styling tools. Opt for natural and herbal hair care products.

6. Medical Conditions & Side Effects of Medications

Certain chronic diseases like diabetes, lupus, autoimmune disorders, and scalp conditions can trigger hair loss. Additionally, medications like chemotherapy drugs, blood pressure medicines, antidepressants, and steroids can cause hair thinning as a side effect.

🔹 Solution: Consult a doctor to explore alternative medications or seek treatment to manage the underlying medical condition.

7. Scalp Infections & Dandruff

Scalp conditions such as dandruff, fungal infections (tinea capitis), psoriasis, and seborrheic dermatitis can cause irritation, itching, and hair fall.

🔹 Solution: Use medicated anti-fungal shampoos and seek medical treatment for scalp infections. A dermatologist can recommend the best solutions for your condition.

8. Smoking & Alcohol Consumption

Excessive smoking and alcohol consumption reduce blood circulation to the scalp, depriving hair follicles of essential nutrients, leading to hair loss.

🔹 Solution: Reduce or quit smoking and drinking to improve overall scalp and hair health.

Dr. Anju Mehra's Expert Advice

According to Dr. Anju Mehra, early intervention is crucial for preventing severe hair loss. If you notice excessive hair shedding, thinning, or bald patches, it's important to consult a dermatologist immediately.

Final Thoughts

Hair loss is a significant concern, but with the right diet, stress management, and proper hair care routine, it can be controlled. With expert advice from Dr. Anju Mehra, Best Dermatologist in Ajmer, you can take the right steps toward healthier, fuller hair.

title: Understanding Vitiligo: Causes, Symptoms, and Treatment Options
content:
Understanding Vitiligo: Causes, Symptoms, and Treatment Options

Vitiligo is more than just a skin condition—it affects millions worldwide, both physically and emotionally. Characterized by the loss of pigment-producing cells, vitiligo results in white patches on the skin. Although non-contagious, it can significantly impact a person's self-esteem and quality of life. Here, we delve into the causes, symptoms, and available treatment options to shed light on this condition and help patients better understand their journey toward healthier skin.

What is Vitiligo?

Vitiligo occurs when melanocytes, the cells responsible for skin pigmentation, are destroyed or stop functioning. This leads to patches of depigmented skin that can appear on any part of the body. The condition can affect people of all skin types and ages, although it is often more noticeable in individuals with darker skin tones due to the stark contrast.

Causes of Vitiligo

The exact cause of vitiligo remains unknown, but several factors are believed to play a role:

  • • Autoimmune Response: The immune system mistakenly attacks melanocytes, leading to their destruction.
  • • Genetics: A family history of vitiligo or other autoimmune conditions can increase the risk.
  • • Environmental Triggers: Factors like sunburn, stress, or exposure to certain chemicals might trigger or worsen the condition.
  • • Neurogenic Factors: Certain chemicals released by nerve endings in the skin can damage melanocytes.

Common Symptoms

The most obvious symptom of vitiligo is the appearance of white or depigmented patches on the skin. These patches often:

  • • Begin on sun-exposed areas, such as the face, hands, and arms.
  • • Spread irregularly over time, although the progression varies from person to person.
  • • May also affect hair, causing premature graying or whitening.
  • • Rarely, involve the eyes, mucous membranes, or inner ear.

Diagnosis of Vitiligo

Diagnosing vitiligo involves a thorough clinical evaluation. A dermatologist may:

  • • Perform a physical exam of the skin.
  • • Use a Wood's lamp (a special UV light) to identify depigmented areas.
  • • Review your medical history for possible autoimmune links.
  • • Conduct skin biopsies or blood tests to rule out other conditions.

Vitiligo Treatment

Treatment Options for Vitiligo

While there is no cure for vitiligo, several treatments can help restore skin color and manage symptoms. The choice of treatment depends on the severity, extent, and location of the patches.

Topical Treatments

  • • Corticosteroid Creams: Can help regain pigment in early stages.
  • • Calcineurin Inhibitors: Useful for sensitive areas like the face or genitals.

Light Therapy

  • • Narrowband UVB Therapy: Stimulates melanocyte activity and slows depigmentation.
  • • Excimer Laser: Targets smaller areas with focused UV light.

title: Best Skin Treatments for Glowing Skin in 2025
content:
Best Skin Treatments for Glowing Skin in 2025

In my clinic, I often see patients who feel overwhelmed by the sheer number of skin treatments available. They come in asking, “Doctor, what is the best treatment?” My answer is always the same: “The best treatment is the one that is best for you.”

A successful treatment plan is not about the latest trend; it's about a correct diagnosis and a tailored strategy. So, let's sit down for a virtual consultation. Based on what you're telling me, here is my professional guidance.

Table of Contents

  • • Dull, tired, and aging skin
  • • Fine lines and wrinkles
  • • Sun spots, melasma, uneven skin tone
  • • Acne scars and pitted scars
  • • Active acne and post-acne marks
  • • Popular laser procedures
  • • Final recommendation

If your main concern is: “My skin looks dull, tired, and lacks its youthful bounce.”

My Assessment: You are experiencing early skin laxity and a slowdown in collagen production. The goal is skin rejuvenation, not just surface-level polishing.

My Treatment Prescription:

  • • First-Line Treatment: Profhilo® 2.0 – This pure hyaluronic acid injection deeply hydrates and stimulates your skin’s natural collagen and elastin production. Two sessions a month apart can give you glowing, firm skin with results lasting up to nine months.
  • • Supporting Therapy: AI-Personalized LED – Customized LED light wavelengths target dullness, reduce inflammation, and energize your skin cells between Profhilo sessions.

If your main concern is: “I'm starting to see fine lines, wrinkles, and my skin isn't as firm as it used to be.”

My Assessment: These are classic signs of intrinsic aging. We need to shift from prevention to active collagen regeneration.

My Treatment Prescription:

  • • Advanced Regeneration: Exosome Therapy – Micro-channels are created using laser or microneedling, then exosomes are applied to instruct your skin to heal itself more efficiently, boosting collagen and smoothing wrinkles naturally.
  • • Combination Approach: Fractional Laser + Exosome Therapy offers powerful rejuvenation for deeper wrinkles.

If your main concern is: “I have sun spots, melasma, or an uneven skin tone.”

My Assessment: You are dealing with hyperpigmentation. This requires targeted energy to break up pigment without damaging surrounding skin.

My Treatment Prescription:

  • • Gold-Standard Treatment: Nanosecond Pico-Laser – Ultra-short pulses shatter melanin clusters, allowing the body to naturally clear them. Dual-wavelength technology treats both superficial and deep pigment for a more even tone.

If your main concern is: “I have acne scars or pitted scars from old injuries.”

My Assessment: Scarring alters the skin's texture and structure. We need to resurface and remodel the collagen in that area.

My Treatment Prescription:

  • • Step 1: Resurface – Ablative or Non-Ablative Fractional Lasers create controlled micro-injuries, breaking up old scar tissue.
  • • Step 2: Rebuild – Exosome Therapy follows immediately, guiding collagen to grow in a healthy, organized pattern, yielding smoother results.

If your main concern is: “I struggle with active acne and the dark marks it leaves behind.”

My Assessment: Acne is a disease and requires treatment for both the active phase and residual scarring.

My Treatment Prescription:

  • • For Active Breakouts: Personalized LED Therapy using blue (anti-bacterial) and red (anti-inflammatory) light helps reduce flare-ups and calm the skin.
  • • For Post-Acne Scars & Marks: Pico-Laser for pigmentation, Profhilo for plumping, and Laser Resurfacing for texture improvement.

A Note on Popular Laser Procedures

  • • Laser Hair Removal: Modern Diode Lasers with skin cooling offer safe, precise, long-lasting results.
  • • Laser Tattoo Removal: Pico-second Lasers minimize scarring and reduce session counts.

My Final Recommendation as Your Doctor

An accurate diagnosis is the foundation of any successful treatment plan. What works for one person may not work for another. In 2025, we have the tools to not only treat your concerns but elevate your skin's overall health. My goal is to help you achieve a radiance that is sustainable and uniquely yours.

title: How to Identify Your Skin Type and Care for It Properly
content:
Find Your Skin Type and Give It the Right Care

Hello, I am Dr. Anju Mehra. In my skin clinic in Ajmer, I meet many people who use wrong products on their skin. They don't know their skin type. Using wrong products can cause many skin problems.

The good news is that knowing your skin type is easy. Once you know it, you can take better care of your skin. Healthy, glowing skin starts with understanding what your skin needs.

Why Knowing Your Skin Type Matters

Your skin is unique. What works for your friend may not work for you. When you know your skin type:

  • You can choose the right products
  • You can prevent skin problems
  • You can save money on wrong products
  • Your skin will look healthier and happier

The Five Main Skin Types

Based on my experience as a dermatologist in Ajmer, I see these five skin types most often:

1. Normal Skin

This is the lucky skin type!

  • How it feels: Not too oily, not too dry
  • Looks like: Clear, smooth, with small pores
  • Care tips: Use mild cleanser and light moisturizer

2. Oily Skin

This skin type makes extra oil.

  • How it feels: Shiny and greasy, especially in T-zone (forehead, nose, chin)
  • Looks like: Large pores, may have blackheads and acne
  • Care tips: Use oil-free products, wash face twice daily

3. Dry Skin

This skin needs more moisture.

  • How it feels: Tight, rough, sometimes flaky
  • Looks like: Dull, with small pores, may have red patches
  • Care tips: Use creamy cleansers, rich moisturizers

4. Combination Skin

This is the most common skin type.

  • How it feels: Oily in T-zone, dry on cheeks
  • Looks like: Shiny forehead and nose, normal or dry cheeks
  • Care tips: Use different products for different areas

5. Sensitive Skin

This skin gets irritated easily.

  • How it feels: Itchy, stinging, or burning
  • Looks like: Redness, may have rashes
  • Care tips: Use gentle, fragrance-free products

Simple Test to Find Your Skin Type

Here's an easy way to know your skin type:

  1. Wash your face with a mild cleanser
  2. Pat dry gently
  3. Don't put any products for one hour
  4. After one hour, look at your skin in mirror

What you see:

  • Shiny all over: Oily skin
  • Flaky or tight: Dry skin
  • Shiny only on T-zone: Combination skin
  • Comfortable and clear: Normal skin
  • Red or itchy: Sensitive skin

Daily Skin Care Routine for Each Skin Type

I have written a detailed guide on daily skin care routine for different skin types. Here are the basics:

For Oily Skin:

  • Use gel-based cleanser
  • Apply oil-free moisturizer
  • Use non-comedogenic products (won't block pores)

For Dry Skin:

  • Use cream-based cleanser
  • Apply thick moisturizer
  • Use hydrating serums

For Combination Skin:

  • Use gentle cleanser
  • Apply light moisturizer all over, extra on dry areas

For Sensitive Skin:

  • Use fragrance-free products
  • Do patch test before using new products
  • Avoid harsh ingredients

Common Mistakes I See in My Ajmer Clinic

Many people make these mistakes:

  • Using wrong products: Oily skin using heavy creams
  • Over-washing: This can make oily skin produce more oil
  • Not using sunscreen: All skin types need sun protection
  • Changing products too often: Give products time to work

When to Visit a Dermatologist in Ajmer

If you have any of these problems, you should come see me at my clinic:

  • Severe acne that doesn't go away
  • Skin that stays red and irritated
  • Dark spots or pigmentation problems
  • Rashes that itch and spread
  • Any sudden change in your skin

Sometimes, you may need professional skin treatments for best results.

My Final Advice

Remember, your skin type can change with age, season, and health. What works now may not work after few years. Listen to your skin and adjust your routine as needed.

Taking care of your skin is not complicated. Start with knowing your skin type, then choose the right products. Be patient and consistent with your skin care routine.

If you need help with your skin, I am here to guide you. At my skin clinic in Ajmer, we help people achieve healthy, beautiful skin every day.

To your beautiful skin,

Dr. Anju Mehra, MD
(Dermatologist & Skin Specialist)

title: Seasonal Skincare Tips: How to Protect Your Skin in Winter
content:
Seasonal Skincare Tips: How to Protect Your Skin in Winter

As a dermatologist in Ajmer, I see a common problem every winter: patients struggling with dry, itchy, and irritated skin. The cold, dry winds of Rajasthan can be particularly harsh on your skin. But don't worry, with a few simple changes to your routine, you can keep your skin healthy, glowing, and comfortable all season long.

Think of your skincare routine like your wardrobe – you need to change it with the seasons. Let's discuss a simple, effective winter skincare plan.

Why Does Winter Affect Your Skin?

Cold air outside and dry heat indoors strip moisture from your skin. This leads to a weakened skin barrier, causing dryness, redness, flakiness, and a feeling of tightness. For those with conditions like eczema or psoriasis, winter can make them worse.

Your Simple Winter Skincare Plan

1. Cleanse Gently, Don't Strip

Swap your foaming or gel-based cleanser for a creamy, hydrating one. Harsh cleansers can remove your skin's natural oils. Look for ingredients like ceramides or glycerin.

  • • Doctor's Tip: Wash your face with lukewarm water, not hot water, as hot water can further dry out your skin.

2. Moisturize, Moisturize, Moisturize!

This is the most important step in winter. A good moisturizer acts like a barrier, locking in hydration. If you need help choosing the right one, learning how to identify your skin type is a great first step.

  • • Look for: Thicker creams or ointments that contain hyaluronic acid, shea butter, or petrolatum.
  • • Doctor's Tip: Apply moisturizer immediately after bathing or washing your face while your skin is still slightly damp. This helps trap the water in your skin.

3. Never Skip Sunscreen

Many people think sunscreen is only for summer. This is a myth! The sun's harmful UV rays are present even on cloudy, cold days. In fact, snow and sand can reflect these rays, increasing exposure. Consistent sun protection is a cornerstone of all anti-aging treatments and healthy skin.

  • • Doctor's Tip: Use a broad-spectrum sunscreen with an SPF of 30 or higher every day, especially on your face, neck, and hands.

4. Use a Humidifier

Indoor heating systems make the air in your home very dry, which sucks moisture from your skin. Using a humidifier in your bedroom at night adds moisture back into the air and can prevent your skin from becoming dry and itchy.

5. Don't Forget Your Lips and Hands

The skin on your lips and hands is thinner and loses moisture faster.

  • • For Lips: Use a fragrance-free lip balm with SPF. Avoid licking your lips, as saliva evaporates and makes them drier.
  • • For Hands: Apply a rich hand cream after every wash. Wear gloves when you go outside.

When to See a Dermatologist in Ajmer

If you follow these tips but still experience severe dryness, cracking, intense itching, or if your eczema or other skin problems flare up, it's time to seek professional help. As your local dermatologist in Ajmer, I can provide prescription-strength creams and advanced skin rejuvenation treatments tailored to your skin's specific needs. For persistent issues like winter acne or significant irritation, a professional consultation is the best step.

My Final Recommendation

Winter skincare doesn't have to be complicated. It's about being consistent, gentle, and proactive with hydration. Protecting your skin barrier is the key to comfortable and healthy skin during the cold months in Rajasthan.

To your health and your glow,

Dr. Anju Mehra, MDYour Trusted Dermatologist in Ajmer

title: How to Get Glowing Skin Fast: A Skin Doctor's Best Tips
content:
How to Get Glowing Skin Fast: A Skin Doctor's Best Tips

Many of my patients ask me for that quick, healthy glow before a big event. I am Dr. Anju Mehra, a skin specialist in Ajmer. The good news is, there are real ways to make your skin look brighter and fresher very quickly.

While healthy skin in the long run needs daily care, here are my top treatments for a fast glow.

What's in this article?

  • Quick Glow Treatments at the Clinic
    • 1. The HydraFacial
    • 2. Chemical Peels
    • 3. Laser or Light Treatment
    • 4. Dermaplaning
  • Your At-Home Glow Routine
  • My Final Advice

Quick Glow Treatments at the Clinic

For the fastest and best results, these are the treatments I do in my clinic.

1. The HydraFacial: A Quick Cleaning for Your Skin

This is one of my favorite treatments for instant glow. It’s a special facial that does four things at once: it cleans, scrubs, clears out pores, and adds moisture.

  • • How it works: A gentle machine sucks out the dirt from your pores and puts in good ingredients like hyaluronic acid.
  • • The Result: Your skin looks clearer, smoother, and shiny right away. It doesn’t hurt, and you can go back to your day immediately.

2. Chemical Peels: A Fresh New Layer

Imagine gently wiping away the top, dull layer of your skin to show the new, bright skin underneath. That’s what a chemical peel does.

  • • How it works: A safe acid solution is put on your skin. It helps to remove old, dead skin cells.
  • • The Result: Your skin looks more even and has a healthy glow. We can make it strong or gentle, depending on your skin.

3. Laser or Light Treatment: For Dark Spots and Dullness

If your skin looks dull because of sun spots, this treatment is great.

  • • How it works: A special laser or light device targets dark spots on your skin to make them lighter.
  • • The Result: Your skin tone becomes more even and bright. You can see a difference after just one session.

4. Dermaplaning: For Super Smooth Skin

Sometimes, the tiny hairs and dead skin on your face can make your skin look dull. Dermaplaning fixes this.

  • • How it works: I use a safe, small tool to gently scrape off the dead skin and peach fuzz from your face.
  • • The Result: Your skin feels incredibly smooth instantly. Light reflects off it better, making it glow. Your makeup and skincare will also go on more smoothly.

Your At-Home Glow Routine

If you can't come to the clinic, here is a simple routine to do at home the night before or the morning of your event.

  1. Wash Well: First, use an oil cleanser to remove makeup and sunscreen. Then, use a gentle water-based face wash. Clean skin is bright skin.
  2. Gentle Scrub (Chemical Exfoliant): Use a mild toner with ingredients like Lactic Acid. Do not use harsh scrubs that can hurt your skin.
  3. Hydrating Mask: Put on a face mask with hyaluronic acid or niacinamide for 15-20 minutes. This will plump up your skin with moisture.
  4. Vitamin C Serum: The next morning, use a Vitamin C serum. It helps to brighten your skin and protect it.
  5. Moisturize and Use Sunscreen: Finish with a good moisturizer. Then, always use sunscreen. Sunscreen is the most important step to prevent dull skin.

My Final Advice

These fast treatments are wonderful, but for skin that always looks good, you need a daily routine. This includes washing your face, using moisturizer, and wearing sunscreen every single day.

Before trying any clinic treatment, please come for a consultation. We can check your skin and choose the best and safest way to get your glow.

Take care of your skin,

Dr. Anju Mehra, MD
Skin and Hair Specialist, Ajmer

title: Dermatologist-Recommended Sunscreens for Indian Skin
content:
Dermatologist-Recommended Sunscreens for Indian Skin

In my practice in Ajmer, one question I hear every single day is, 'Doctor, which sunscreen is best for me?' As an Indian dermatologist, I understand our unique skin needs—the tendency towards pigmentation, the challenge of oily skin in this humid climate, and the constant battle against the strong Indian sun. I am Dr. Anju Mehra, and today, I will simplify the process of choosing the right sunscreen for your Indian skin.

Think of sunscreen not as a cosmetic product, but as the most crucial part of your skincare—your primary shield against aging, dark spots, and skin damage.

What's in this article?

  • Why is Sunscreen Non-Negotiable for Indian Skin?
  • What to Look for in a Sunscreen
    • 1. Broad-Spectrum Protection
    • 2. The Right SPF
    • 3. Texture Matters
  • My Top Sunscreen Recommendations
    • For Oily & Acne-Prone Skin
    • For Dry & Sensitive Skin
    • For Combination Skin
    • For Sensitive Skin & Children (Mineral Sunscreens)
  • Common Sunscreen Mistakes to Avoid
  • My Final Prescription

Why is Sunscreen Non-Negotiable for Indian Skin?

Our skin has more melanin, which offers some natural protection, but it is not enough. The intense UVA and UVB rays in our climate are the main cause of:

  • • Tanning and Pigmentation: The most common complaint I see.
  • • Premature Aging: Wrinkles, fine lines, and loss of elasticity.
  • • Worsening of Melasma and PIH (Post-Inflammatory Hyperpigmentation).
  • • Increased Risk of Skin Cancer.

What to Look for in a Sunscreen

Don't just pick any bottle off the shelf. Look for these three key features.

1. Broad-Spectrum Protection

This is the most important term. Broad-spectrum means the sunscreen protects from both UVA (aging rays) and UVB (burning rays). In India, look for a high PA++++ rating, which indicates the best UVA protection.

2. The Right SPF

For daily use in India, SPF 30 to 50 is ideal. SPF 30 blocks about 97% of UVB rays, while SPF 50 blocks about 98%. There is no need to go for extremely high SPF (like 100) as the marginal benefit is negligible, and it can feel heavy and greasy.

3. Texture Matters: Gel, Lotion, or Cream?

  • • Oily Skin: Choose gel-based, matte-finish, or water-based sunscreens. They are non-comedogenic (won't clog pores).
  • • Dry Skin: Opt for lotions or creams that contain hydrating ingredients like Hyaluronic Acid or Ceramides.
  • • Sensitive Skin: Mineral or Physical Sunscreens with Zinc Oxide or Titanium Dioxide are less likely to cause irritation.

My Top Sunscreen Recommendations

Based on what works well for my patients in our climate, here are some reliable categories and brands.

For Oily & Acne-Prone Skin

  • • Neutrogena Ultra Sheer Dry-Touch Sunscreen: A classic. It gives a matte finish and feels light.
  • • La Shield Sunscreen Gel: A dermatologist-favorite, especially the Mineral one. It's non-greasy and very effective.
  • • Aqualogica Glow+ Dewy Sunscreen: A good hydrating yet lightweight option that doesn't feel heavy.

For Dry & Sensitive Skin

  • • Blynds Emulgel: A fantastic pharmacy product that is hypoallergenic and great for sensitive skin.
  • • Re'equil Ultra Matte Dry Touch Sunscreen: Though matte, it works well for dry skin types that need strong sun protection without irritation.
  • • The Derma Co. Hyaluronic Sunscreen Aqua Gel: Provides hydration along with sun protection.

For Combination Skin

  • • Lotus Herbals Safe Sun UV Screen: A wide range of textures are available to suit different preferences.
  • • Fixderma Shadow SPF 50+ Gel: A lightweight gel that works for most skin types without feeling sticky.

For Sensitive Skin & Children (Mineral Sunscreens)

  • • QV Face Moisturising Day Cream: A gentle, mineral-based option.
  • • IPCA Acne-UV Gel SPF 50: While a chemical sunscreen, it is specifically formulated for sensitive, acne-prone skin and is a staple in many dermatology clinics.

Common Sunscreen Mistakes to Avoid

  1. Using Too Little: You need a full teaspoon for your face and neck. Less than that drastically reduces protection.
  2. Skipping Reapplication: Sunscreen degrades. Reapply every 3-4 hours, and immediately after swimming or heavy sweating.
  3. Using Expired Sunscreen: Check the expiration date. An old sunscreen loses its effectiveness.
  4. Using Sunscreen Only Outdoors: UVA rays can penetrate windows. Make sunscreen a part of your morning routine, even if you are indoors.

My Final Prescription

The best sunscreen is the one you will use happily every single day. Don't be afraid to try a few to find the texture and finish you love.

If you have specific skin conditions like melasma, extreme sensitivity, or active acne, a personalized recommendation is always best. Please book a consultation with me, and we can find your perfect sun shield together.

Protect your skin, preserve its health,

Dr. Anju Mehra, MDSkin and Hair Specialist, Ajmer

title: Winter Skin Care Tips for Dry Skin - Dr. Anju Mehra
content:
Winter Skin Care Tips for Dry Skin - Expert Advice by Dr. Anju Mehra

Winter in Rajasthan can be particularly harsh on your skin. The cold, dry winds combined with indoor heating can leave your skin feeling tight, flaky, and uncomfortable. As a dermatologist at my skin clinic in Ajmer, I see many patients struggling with dry skin during these months. But don't worry – with the right care, you can keep your skin soft, hydrated, and healthy all winter long.

What's in this article?

  • • Why Winter Makes Dry Skin Worse
  • • Simple Winter Skin Care Routine
  • • Special Care for Extra Dry Areas
  • • Lifestyle Changes for Winter Skin
  • • When to See a Dermatologist

Why Does Winter Make Dry Skin Worse?

Cold air outside and dry heat indoors strip moisture from your skin. This damages your skin's natural protective barrier, leading to:

  • • Flakiness and rough texture
  • • Itching and irritation
  • • Redness and inflammation
  • • Cracks in severe cases

Simple Winter Skin Care Routine for Dry Skin

1. Gentle Cleansing is Key

Switch to a creamy, hydrating cleanser instead of foaming ones that can strip natural oils. If you're unsure about your skin type, our guide can help you identify it properly.

  • • Doctor's Tip: Use lukewarm water, not hot water, for washing your face and bathing
  • • Doctor's Tip: Limit bath time to 10-15 minutes maximum

2. Lock in Moisture Immediately

Apply moisturizer within 3 minutes of bathing while your skin is still damp. For severe dryness, consider our professional skin rejuvenation treatments.

  • • Look for: Creams with ceramides, hyaluronic acid, shea butter, or glycerin
  • • Avoid: Alcohol-based products that can dry out skin further

3. Don't Skip Sunscreen

Many people think sunscreen is only for summer, but winter sun can be just as damaging. Check out our detailed guide on dermatologist-recommended sunscreens for Indian skin.

  • • Use broad-spectrum SPF 30+ daily
  • • Choose moisturizing sunscreens with hydrating ingredients

4. Weekly Hydrating Treatments

Give your skin extra love with these simple home treatments. For more home remedies for skin problems, visit our comprehensive guide.

  • • Honey Mask: Apply raw honey for 15 minutes – it's naturally moisturizing and antibacterial
  • • Oatmeal Soak: Add colloidal oatmeal to your bath for instant relief from itching

Special Care for Extra Dry Areas

For Extremely Dry Hands:

  • • Apply thick hand cream after every wash
  • • Wear gloves when going outside
  • • Use cotton gloves overnight with moisturizer for deep treatment

For Chapped Lips:

  • • Use fragrance-free lip balm with SPF
  • • Avoid licking your lips – it makes dryness worse
  • • Gently exfoliate with a soft toothbrush once a week

Lifestyle Changes for Winter Skin Health

1. Use a Humidifier

Combat dry indoor air by using a humidifier in your bedroom at night. Aim for 40-60% humidity.

2. Stay Hydrated

Drink plenty of water and warm fluids throughout the day. Herbal teas and warm water with lemon are great options.

3. Choose Skin-Friendly Fabrics

Wear soft, natural fabrics like cotton and silk next to your skin instead of wool, which can be irritating.

4. Eat for Skin Health

Include foods rich in omega-3 fatty acids (like walnuts, flaxseeds) and antioxidants (fruits and vegetables) in your diet. For more tips on achieving glowing skin, read our expert guide.

When to See a Dermatologist

If you experience any of these symptoms, it's time to book a consultation with me at my skin clinic in Ajmer:

  • • Severe itching that disturbs sleep
  • • Cracked skin that bleeds
  • • Red, inflamed patches
  • • No improvement after 2 weeks of proper care

My Final Winter Skin Care Prescription

Remember, consistency is key when dealing with dry winter skin. Stick to your routine, be gentle with your skin, and don't hesitate to seek professional help if needed. For more seasonal skincare tips, explore our winter care guide.

With these simple tips, you can enjoy comfortable, healthy skin throughout the winter season. Your skin deserves the best care, especially during these challenging months.

Stay warm and hydrated,

Dr. Anju Mehra, MD
Dermatologist & Skin Specialist

title: How to Remove Pimple Marks and Dark Spots Naturally at Home - Dermatologist Approved
content:
How to Remove Pimple Marks and Dark Spots Naturally at Home - Dermatologist Approved

As a dermatologist practicing in Ajmer, I understand how frustrating it can be to deal with stubborn pimple marks and dark spots long after the acne has cleared. Many of my patients come to my skin clinic in Ajmer seeking solutions for post-acne pigmentation. While we offer advanced laser treatments for severe cases, there are several effective natural remedies you can try at home first. I'm Dr. Anju Mehra, and in this comprehensive guide, I'll share scientifically-backed natural treatments that actually work.

What's in this article?

  • Understanding Different Types of Acne Marks
  • Top 7 Dermatologist-Approved Natural Remedies
    • 1. Aloe Vera - The Ultimate Skin Healer
    • 2. Vitamin E Oil - The Repair Specialist
      • 3. Rosehip Oil - Natural Retinol Alternative
    • 4. Green Tea Extract - Antioxidant Powerhouse
    • 5. Licorice Root Extract - Gentle Lightener
    • 6. Niacinamide-Rich Foods
    • 7. Sandalwood & Multani Mitti - Traditional Wisdom
  • 4-Week Natural Clearing Protocol
  • Dietary Tips to Speed Up Healing
  • How to Prevent New Marks from Forming
  • When Natural Remedies Aren't Enough

Understanding Different Types of Acne Marks

Before we begin treatment, it's crucial to understand what type of marks you're dealing with:

  • • Post-Inflammatory Hyperpigmentation (PIH): Flat, dark spots caused by excess melanin production during inflammation. These respond well to natural treatments.
  • • Post-Inflammatory Erythema (PIE): Red or pink marks caused by dilated blood vessels. These require different treatment approaches.
  • • Atrophic Scars: Depressed or pitted scars that indicate collagen loss. These need professional scar treatment.

Top 7 Dermatologist-Approved Natural Remedies

1. Aloe Vera - The Ultimate Skin Healer

Aloe vera is my top recommendation for its remarkable healing properties and scientific backing.

  • • Scientific Evidence: Contains aloin and aloesin, compounds that inhibit melanin production
  • • How to use: Apply fresh aloe gel directly from plant, leave for 30 minutes, rinse with cool water
  • • Frequency: Twice daily for best results
  • • Best for: All skin types, especially sensitive skin

2. Vitamin E Oil - The Repair Specialist

Vitamin E is a powerful antioxidant that helps repair skin damage and lighten dark spots.

  • • Scientific Evidence: Studies show vitamin E accelerates wound healing and reduces oxidative stress
  • • How to use: Puncture vitamin E capsule, mix with 1 tsp aloe vera gel, apply overnight
  • • Frequency: Nightly treatment 3-4 times weekly
  • • Tip: Do patch test first - can be heavy for oily skin

3. Rosehip Oil - Natural Retinol Alternative

Rosehip oil is rich in trans-retinoic acid, a natural form of vitamin A that helps with cell turnover.

  • • Scientific Evidence: Clinical studies show improvement in post-acne scars and pigmentation
  • • How to use: Apply 2-3 drops on damp skin, gently massage into marks
  • • Frequency: Daily nighttime use
  • • Best for: Dry to normal skin types

4. Green Tea Extract - Antioxidant Powerhouse

Green tea is packed with EGCG, a potent antioxidant that reduces inflammation and lightens pigmentation.

  • • Scientific Evidence: EGCG inhibits melanogenesis and reduces inflammation
  • • How to use: Brew strong green tea, refrigerate, use as toner twice daily
  • • Alternative: Mix used green tea leaves with honey as weekly mask
  • • Best for: Oily and acne-prone skin

5. Licorice Root Extract - Gentle Lightener

Licorice root contains glabridin, which inhibits tyrosinase activity without the irritation of hydroquinone.

  • • Scientific Evidence: Glabridin is 16 times more effective than hydroquinone in some studies
  • • How to use: Make licorice root tea, use as spray or mix with aloe vera
  • • Frequency: Daily use as toner
  • • Best for: Sensitive skin that can't tolerate strong lighteners

6. Niacinamide-Rich Foods and Applications

Niacinamide (Vitamin B3) is clinically proven to reduce hyperpigmentation and improve skin barrier.

  • • Natural Sources: Rice water, yeast, whole grains
  • • How to use: Soak rice in water for 30 minutes, use water as face rinse
  • • Frequency: Daily as final rinse after cleansing
  • • Evidence: 5% niacinamide shows significant improvement in hyperpigmentation in 8 weeks

7. Sandalwood & Multani Mitti - Traditional Wisdom

This traditional combination has stood the test of time for good reason.

  • • How to use: Mix sandalwood powder, multani mitti, rose water, and turmeric into paste
  • • Application: Apply even layer, leave until dry (15-20 minutes), rinse with cool water
  • • Frequency: 2-3 times weekly
  • • Benefits: Exfoliates, lightens, and reduces inflammation simultaneously

4-Week Natural Clearing Protocol

For optimal results, follow this structured approach:

  • • Weeks 1-2: Focus on anti-inflammatory treatments (aloe vera, green tea) to calm skin
  • • Weeks 3-4: Introduce active lightening agents (rosehip oil, licorice extract)
  • • Daily Routine: Morning - rice water rinse, green tea toner; Night - aloe vera, rosehip oil
  • • Weekly Treatments: Sandalwood mask (Wednesday), Vitamin E overnight (Saturday)

Dietary Tips to Speed Up Healing from Within

What you eat significantly impacts how quickly your skin heals. Include these in your diet:

  • • Vitamin C-rich foods: Amla, oranges, bell peppers - boost collagen production
  • • Zinc sources: Pumpkin seeds, lentils, chickpeas - reduce inflammation
  • • Omega-3 fatty acids: Flaxseeds, walnuts - improve skin barrier function
  • • Antioxidant-rich foods: Berries, green tea, dark chocolate - combat oxidative stress

How to Prevent New Marks from Forming

Prevention is always better than cure. Follow these essential steps:

  • • Never pop pimples: This is the number one cause of marks and acne scars
  • • Daily sunscreen: UV exposure darkens existing marks. Use our recommended sunscreens for Indian skin
  • • Treat acne early: Don't wait for breakouts to become severe
  • • Gentle skincare: Avoid harsh scrubs that can cause micro-tears and inflammation

When Natural Remedies Aren't Enough

While these natural treatments are effective for mild to moderate cases, consult a dermatologist if:

  • • No improvement after 8-12 weeks of consistent natural treatment
  • • You have deep pitted scars rather than surface pigmentation
  • • Marks are affecting your mental health and confidence
  • • You're dealing with active cystic acne along with marks

At my dermatology clinic in Ajmer, we offer advanced treatments like laser skin resurfacing, medical-grade peels, and prescription treatments for stubborn cases that don't respond to natural remedies.

My Final Professional Advice

Patience and consistency are crucial when using natural remedies. Most pimple marks take 3-6 months to fade completely, even with professional treatment. Remember to:

  • • Always do patch tests before trying new ingredients
  • • Take weekly photos to track progress
  • • Combine topical treatments with internal nutrition
  • • Protect your skin from sun exposure every single day

Your skin has an incredible ability to heal when given the right support. Start with these natural remedies, be consistent, and don't hesitate to seek professional help if needed.

To clear, confident skin,

Dr. Anju Mehra, MD
Dermatologist & Skin Specialist

title: Acne Treatment at Home: Simple Remedies That Actually Work
content:
Acne Treatment at Home: Simple Remedies That Actually Work

Are you tired of dealing with pimples and acne? You're not alone! Many people in Ajmer and across India struggle with acne. The good news is that you don't always need expensive treatments. As a dermatologist in Ajmer, I often recommend simple home remedies that can help clear your skin naturally.

I'm Dr. Anju Mehra, and in this guide, I'll share easy, effective home treatments for acne that you can try today. Remember, for severe acne, it's always best to visit a skin specialist in Ajmer for proper treatment.

Why Do We Get Acne?

First, let's understand what causes acne:

  • • Oil and dirt clog pores: When oil mixes with dead skin cells, it blocks your pores
  • • Bacteria growth: Blocked pores create perfect conditions for bacteria
  • • Hormonal changes: Especially common during teenage years
  • • Poor diet and stress: Can make acne worse

Top 5 Home Remedies for Acne

1. Aloe Vera - Nature's Healing Gel

Aloe vera is one of the best natural treatments for acne. It helps in many ways:

  • • Reduces redness and swelling
  • • Fights bacteria that cause pimples
  • • Speeds up healing of acne marks

How to use: Take fresh aloe vera gel from the plant. Apply directly on pimples. Leave for 20 minutes, then wash with cool water. Do this twice daily.

2. Honey and Cinnamon Mask

This simple kitchen remedy works wonders for acne-prone skin:

  • • Honey fights bacteria naturally
  • • Cinnamon reduces inflammation
  • • Together they help clear pimples fast

How to use: Mix 2 tablespoons of raw honey with 1 teaspoon of cinnamon powder. Apply on clean face. Leave for 10-15 minutes. Rinse with lukewarm water. Use once a week.

3. Tea Tree Oil - Natural Antiseptic

Tea tree oil is excellent for fighting acne-causing bacteria. Many of my patients at our Ajmer skin clinic find it very effective.

  • • Kills acne bacteria effectively
  • • Reduces swelling and redness
  • • Helps prevent new breakouts

Important: Never use pure tea tree oil directly on skin! Always dilute it.

How to use: Mix 1-2 drops of tea tree oil with 1 tablespoon of water or aloe vera gel. Apply only on pimples with cotton swab. Use once daily.

4. Green Tea Face Wash

Green tea isn't just good for drinking - it's great for your skin too!

  • • Rich in antioxidants that fight inflammation
  • • Reduces excess oil production
  • • Gentle on sensitive skin

How to use: Brew green tea and let it cool. Use it as a face wash in the morning. You can also soak green tea bags and place them on pimples for 10 minutes.

5. Turmeric and Yogurt Pack

This traditional remedy has been used for centuries in Indian homes:

  • • Turmeric has anti-inflammatory properties
  • • Yogurt contains lactic acid that gently exfoliates
  • • Together they reduce acne and lighten marks

How to use: Mix 1 teaspoon turmeric powder with 2 tablespoons plain yogurt. Apply on face for 15-20 minutes. Rinse well. Use twice a week.

Daily Skin Care Routine for Acne-Prone Skin

Along with these remedies, follow this simple daily routine:

  1. Morning: Wash face with mild cleanser → Apply aloe vera gel → Use oil-free moisturizer → Apply sunscreen
  2. Evening: Remove makeup (if any) → Wash face with cleanser → Apply acne treatment → Use light moisturizer
  3. Weekly: Use honey-cinnamon mask or turmeric-yogurt pack once a week

What NOT to Do When You Have Acne

As a dermatologist in Ajmer, I see many patients making these common mistakes:

  • • Don't pop pimples: This causes scars and spreads bacteria
  • • Don't use harsh soaps: They strip natural oils and make acne worse
  • • Don't touch your face often: Your hands carry dirt and bacteria
  • • Don't skip moisturizer: Even oily skin needs hydration

When to See a Dermatologist in Ajmer

Home remedies work well for mild acne. But you should visit a skin specialist if:

  • • Your acne is severe and painful
  • • Home remedies don't work after 4-6 weeks
  • • You're getting deep, cystic acne
  • • Acne is leaving dark spots and scars

At our skin clinic in Ajmer, we offer various acne treatments including medications, chemical peels, and laser treatments for stubborn cases.

Diet Tips for Clear Skin

What you eat affects your skin. Try these dietary changes:

  • • Drink plenty of water: Aim for 8-10 glasses daily
  • • Eat fresh fruits and vegetables: Especially vitamin-rich foods
  • • Reduce sugar and junk food: These can trigger breakouts
  • • Include nuts and seeds: Good for skin health

Final Thoughts

Dealing with acne can be frustrating, but with patience and the right approach, you can achieve clear skin. Start with these simple home remedies and give them time to work - usually 4-8 weeks.

Remember, everyone's skin is different. What works for one person may not work for another. Be consistent with your routine, and don't get discouraged if results take time.

If your acne doesn't improve with home treatment, I'm here to help at my dermatology clinic in Ajmer. We can create a personalized treatment plan for your specific skin needs.

To your clear, healthy skin,

Dr. Anju Mehra, MD
Dermatologist & Skin Specialist, Ajmer

title: How Diet Affects Skin Health - A Dermatologist's Guide
content:
How Diet Affects Skin Health - A Dermatologist's Guide

As a dermatologist in Ajmer, many patients ask me: "Doctor, can my food choices really change my skin?" The answer is yes! What you eat directly impacts how your skin looks and feels. Your skin is a mirror of your inner health, and today, I'll explain how simple diet changes can lead to glowing, healthy skin.

I'm Dr. Anju Mehra, and in this simple guide, I'll show you how to eat for better skin health.

What's in this article?

  • The Skin-Food Connection: How It Works
  • Best Foods for Healthy Skin
  • Foods That Can Harm Your Skin
  • Diet Solutions for Common Skin Problems
  • Simple 7-Day Skin-Healthy Eating Plan
  • Why Water is Your Skin's Best Friend

The Skin-Food Connection: How It Works

Think of your skin as a plant. Just like plants need good soil and water to grow, your skin needs proper nutrition to stay healthy. When you eat well:

  • • Your body gets building blocks for new skin cells
  • • Inflammation in your body reduces
  • • Your skin can fight damage better
  • • Healing happens faster

Best Foods for Healthy Skin

1. Vitamin C Rich Foods - For Glowing Skin

Vitamin C helps make collagen, which keeps your skin firm and young-looking. This is especially important if you're considering anti-aging treatments.

  • • Eat: Oranges, lemons, amla, bell peppers, strawberries
  • • Simple tip: Start your day with lemon water

2. Omega-3 Foods - For Soft, Hydrated Skin

These healthy fats keep your skin moist from inside and reduce inflammation, which is perfect for those with dry skin issues.

  • • Eat: Walnuts, flax seeds, chia seeds, fish (if non-vegetarian)
  • • Simple tip: Add 1 tablespoon flax seeds to your breakfast

3. Zinc Rich Foods - For Acne Control

Zinc helps control oil production and fights bacteria that cause pimples. This complements professional acne treatments we offer at our clinic.

  • • Eat: Pumpkin seeds, chickpeas, lentils, whole grains
  • • Simple tip: Snack on roasted pumpkin seeds

4. Vitamin E Foods - For Skin Protection

Vitamin E protects your skin from sun damage and keeps it smooth. Along with using proper sunscreen, this helps prevent skin damage.

  • • Eat: Almonds, sunflower seeds, spinach, avocado
  • • Simple tip: Eat 5-6 almonds daily (soaked overnight)

5. Probiotic Foods - For Clear Skin

Good gut health means good skin health. Probiotics balance your gut bacteria, which can help with various skin problems.

  • • Eat: Yogurt, buttermilk, fermented foods
  • • Simple tip: Have a bowl of fresh yogurt with meals

Foods That Can Harm Your Skin

Just as some foods help your skin, others can cause problems. Understanding this is key when learning how to care for your skin type.

  • • Sugar and sweets: Can increase inflammation and oil production
  • • Fried and oily foods: May worsen acne in some people
  • • Too much dairy: Can trigger breakouts in acne-prone skin
  • • Processed foods: Lack nutrients your skin needs

Remember: You don't need to completely avoid these foods. Just eat them in smaller amounts.

Diet Solutions for Common Skin Problems

For Acne and Pimples:

  • • Eat more zinc-rich foods (pumpkin seeds, lentils)
  • • Reduce sugar and fried foods
  • • Drink green tea - it has anti-inflammatory benefits

For Dry Skin:

If you're following our winter skin care tips, combine them with these diet changes:

  • • Increase omega-3 foods (walnuts, flax seeds)
  • • Eat healthy fats like avocado and ghee in moderation
  • • Include cucumber, watermelon in your diet

For Dull Skin and Dark Circles:

  • • Eat iron-rich foods (spinach, dates, beetroot)
  • • Include vitamin C foods to help iron absorption
  • • Stay hydrated - drink enough water

For Aging Skin and Wrinkles:

Along with considering skin rejuvenation treatments, include these foods:

  • • Eat berries - they're full of antioxidants
  • • Include tomatoes - they have lycopene for sun protection
  • • Eat colored vegetables - the more colors, the better

Simple 7-Day Skin-Healthy Eating Plan

You don't need to make big changes. Start with this easy plan:

  • • Morning: Lemon water + 5-6 soaked almonds
  • • Breakfast: Bowl of fresh fruits or vegetable poha
  • • Lunch: Whole grain roti + dal + green vegetables + salad
  • • Evening: Green tea + handful of nuts
  • • Dinner: Light meal like khichdi or vegetable soup
  • • Before bed: Glass of warm milk with turmeric

Why Water is Your Skin's Best Friend

No discussion about diet and skin is complete without talking about water. When you drink enough water:

  • • Your skin stays hydrated from inside
  • • Toxins get flushed out of your body
  • • Your skin looks plump and fresh

How much water? Try to drink 8-10 glasses daily. If you forget, keep a water bottle with you.

When to See a Dermatologist

While diet helps, some skin problems need medical treatment. Visit my skin clinic in Ajmer if:

  • • Your skin problems don't improve with diet changes
  • • You have severe acne that needs professional acne treatment
  • • You need personalized advice for your skin type
  • • You want to combine diet with professional skin treatments

For stubborn skin issues like pimple marks and dark spots that don't respond to diet, we offer advanced treatments at our clinic.

My Final Advice

Remember, good skin starts from within. You don't need expensive creams or complicated diets. Just follow these simple tips:

  1. Eat more fruits and vegetables - aim for different colors
  2. Include nuts and seeds in your daily diet
  3. Drink enough water throughout the day
  4. Reduce sugar and processed foods
  5. Be patient - skin changes take 4-8 weeks to show

Your skin is worth the effort. Start with one small change today - maybe add some fruits to your breakfast or drink an extra glass of water. Small steps lead to big improvements in your skin health.

For those looking for quick results, combine diet with our tips on how to get glowing skin fast.

To your healthy, glowing skin,

Dr. Anju Mehra, MD
Dermatologist & Skin Specialist, Ajmer

title: Morning vs Night Skincare Routine: What Works Best for Indian Skin?
content:
Morning vs Night Skincare Routine: What Works Best for Indian Skin?

As a dermatologist in Ajmer, I often see patients confused about skincare routines. "Should my morning and night routines be different?" is one of the most common questions. For Indian skin, which faces unique challenges like pollution, humidity, and pigmentation tendencies, getting this right is crucial. Let me simplify this for you.

I'm Dr. Anju Mehra, and in this guide, I'll break down exactly what your skin needs in the morning versus at night for that healthy, glowing complexion.

What's in this article?

  • Why Morning & Night Routines MUST Be Different
  • The Perfect Morning Routine for Indian Skin
  • The Essential Night Routine for Indian Skin
  • Routines for Your Specific Skin Type
  • Common Mistakes to Avoid
  • Simple Product Guide for Indian Skin
  • Weekly Treatments to Boost Results

Why Morning & Night Routines MUST Be Different

Think of it this way: Your morning routine is like putting on armor for battle (against sun, pollution, stress), while your night routine is like healing and repairing in a safe camp. Your skin has different needs at different times:

  • • Morning: Protection, hydration, defense against daily damage
  • • Night: Repair, regeneration, treatment of specific concerns
  • • Indian Skin Specific: Our skin tends to be more prone to tanning, pigmentation, and oiliness, requiring tailored approaches

The Perfect Morning Routine for Indian Skin (5 Steps)

Step 1: Gentle Cleansing (1 minute)

Start fresh without stripping natural oils. Unlike heavy night cleansing, morning cleansing should be light.

  • • Use: Mild, pH-balanced face wash
  • • For oily skin: Gel-based cleanser
  • • For dry skin: Cream or milk cleanser
  • • Avoid: Harsh soaps that disrupt skin barrier

Step 2: Vitamin C Serum (30 seconds)

The superstar of morning routines! Vitamin C provides antioxidant protection against daily environmental damage.

  • • Why: Fights free radicals from pollution and UV rays
  • • Bonus: Brightens skin and reduces pigmentation
  • • Application: 3-4 drops on slightly damp skin

Step 3: Moisturizer (30 seconds)

Yes, even oily skin needs morning moisture! This creates a barrier and prevents excess oil production.

  • • For oily/combination: Lightweight, oil-free, non-comedogenic moisturizer
  • • For dry skin: Richer cream with ceramides or hyaluronic acid
  • • Pro tip: Apply while skin is still damp from serum

Step 4: SUNSCREEN - The Non-Negotiable (1 minute)

This is your most important morning step. For Indian skin prone to tanning and pigmentation, sunscreen is non-negotiable. Check our guide on dermatologist-recommended sunscreens.

  • • SPF: Minimum SPF 30, preferably SPF 50
  • • Type: Broad spectrum (protects against UVA & UVB)
  • • Amount: Two finger lengths for face and neck
  • • Reapplication: Every 3-4 hours if outdoors

Step 5: Lip Protection (10 seconds)

Often forgotten but crucial for Indian conditions.

  • • Use: SPF lip balm
  • • Extra: Light lip tint if desired

The Essential Night Routine for Indian Skin (6 Steps)

Step 1: Double Cleansing (2-3 minutes)

This is CRITICAL for Indian skin exposed to pollution and makeup.

  • • First cleanse: Oil-based cleanser or micellar water to remove sunscreen, makeup, pollution
  • • Second cleanse: Water-based cleanser to clean pores
  • • Why: Single cleansing won't remove all the day's grime

Step 2: Toner (Optional but Helpful) (30 seconds)

Restores pH balance and prepares skin for treatment products.

  • • Choose: Alcohol-free, hydrating toners
  • • Avoid: Astringent toners that dry out Indian skin
  • • Alternative: Rose water works beautifully

Step 3: Treatment Products (1-2 minutes)

This is where you address specific concerns. Night is when your skin repairs itself, making it the perfect time for actives.

  • • For acne/breakouts: Salicylic acid or benzoyl peroxide spot treatment
  • • For pigmentation/dark spots: Products with ingredients like alpha arbutin, kojic acid, or prescription hydroquinone
  • • For anti-aging: Retinol or retinoids (start slow, 1-2 times weekly)
  • • For hydration: Hyaluronic acid serum on damp skin

Step 4: Eye Cream (30 seconds)

The delicate eye area needs special attention, especially for preventing dark circles common in Indian skin.

  • • Application: Pat gently with ring finger
  • • Ingredients: Look for caffeine (for puffiness), vitamin C (for brightness), peptides (for fine lines)

Step 5: Moisturizer or Night Cream (30 seconds)

Night moisturizers can be richer since you're not going out in the sun.

  • • For all skin types: Look for ceramides, niacinamide, peptides
  • • For dry skin: Consider facial oils or sleeping masks 1-2 times weekly
  • • Pro tip: Apply to slightly damp skin to lock in moisture

Step 6: Lip Treatment (10 seconds)

  • • Use: Thicker lip balm or treatment
  • • Ingredients: Look for lanolin, shea butter, vitamin E

Routines for Your Specific Skin Type

For Oily/Acne-Prone Indian Skin:

If you struggle with acne, combine this routine with professional acne treatments.

  • • Morning: Salicylic acid cleanser, oil-free moisturizer, matte sunscreen
  • • Night: Double cleanse, niacinamide serum, lightweight gel moisturizer
  • • Weekly: Clay mask to control oil

For Dry Indian Skin:

Especially important during winter months.

  • • Morning: Cream cleanser, hyaluronic acid serum, rich moisturizer, sunscreen
  • • Night: Oil cleanse, hydrating toner, ceramide cream, facial oil if needed
  • • Weekly: Hydrating sheet masks

For Combination Indian Skin:

Most common among Indians - oily T-zone, dry cheeks.

  • • Strategy: Treat different areas differently
  • • Morning: Gel cleanser, lightweight moisturizer all over, extra hydration on dry areas
  • • Night: Double cleanse, different treatments for different zones if needed

For Sensitive Indian Skin:

  • • Morning: Ultra-gentle cleanser, minimal products, physical sunscreen (zinc oxide/titanium dioxide)
  • • Night: Gentle cleansing, calming ingredients like centella asiatica, fragrance-free moisturizer
  • • Patch test: Always test new products

Common Mistakes to Avoid with Indian Skin

In my practice at our skin clinic in Ajmer, I see these mistakes repeatedly:

  • • Using the same routine morning and night: Your skin has different needs!
  • • Skipping sunscreen indoors: UVA rays penetrate windows
  • • Over-exfoliating: Indian skin can easily become irritated
  • • Using too many actives at once: Leads to barrier damage
  • • Not removing sunscreen properly at night: Causes breakouts
  • • Changing products too frequently: Give them 4-6 weeks to work

Simple Product Guide for Indian Skin

You don't need expensive products. Look for these ingredients:

Morning Essentials:

  • • Cleanser: pH balanced, sulfate-free
  • • Vitamin C: L-ascorbic acid (10-20%), stable formulation
  • • Moisturizer: Non-comedogenic, appropriate for your skin type
  • • Sunscreen: Broad spectrum, SPF 30+, suitable for daily use

Night Essentials:

  • • First cleanser: Oil-based or micellar water
  • • Second cleanser: Gentle foaming or cream cleanser
  • • Treatment: Based on your concern (retinol, acne treatment, brightening serum)
  • • Moisturizer: Repair-focused with ceramides, peptides

Weekly Treatments to Boost Results

Add these 1-2 times weekly for extra benefits:

  • • Exfoliation: Chemical exfoliants (AHAs like glycolic acid) for dullness and texture
  • • Masks: Clay masks for oily skin, hydrating masks for dry skin
  • • Face massage: Improves circulation and product absorption
  • • Professional treatments: Consider occasional skin rejuvenation treatments for deeper concerns

When to See a Dermatologist

While a good routine helps, professional guidance is sometimes necessary. Visit our clinic in Ajmer if:

  • • Your skin concerns aren't improving with OTC products
  • • You need prescription-strength treatments for acne, pigmentation, or aging
  • • You want personalized advice for your specific skin type and concerns
  • • You're considering combining home care with professional skin treatments

My Final Advice for Indian Skin

Remember these key principles:

  1. Morning = Protect: Sunscreen is non-negotiable, antioxidants are your friend
  2. Night = Repair: Cleanse thoroughly, treat specific concerns, moisturize well
  3. Consistency beats perfection: A simple routine done consistently is better than a complicated one done occasionally
  4. Listen to your skin: Indian skin changes with seasons, stress, diet - adjust accordingly
  5. Be patient: Skin changes take 4-8 weeks minimum

Start tonight with proper double cleansing and tomorrow morning with sunscreen application. These two changes alone will transform your skin.

For more tips on getting that glow, check our guide on how to get glowing skin fast.

To your healthy, radiant skin,

Dr. Anju Mehra, MD
Dermatologist & Skin Specialist, Ajmer

title: Hormonal Acne: Causes & Treatment - Dermatologist Guide
content:
Hormonal Acne: Causes & Treatment - Expert Guide by Dr. Anju Mehra

Hello, I am Dr. Anju Mehra, a dermatologist in Ajmer. In my practice, I meet many patients who struggle with acne that comes and goes, especially around their chin, jawline, and cheeks. Often, this is hormonal acne - a common problem that needs special understanding and treatment.

If you've tried regular acne treatments at home without success, or if your breakouts follow a monthly pattern, this guide is for you. Let me explain hormonal acne in simple terms and show you how we can treat it effectively at our skin clinic in Ajmer.

What's in this article?

  • What is Hormonal Acne?
  • Common Signs of Hormonal Acne
  • Main Causes of Hormonal Acne
  • Who Gets Hormonal Acne?
  • Treatment Options for Hormonal Acne
  • Helpful Home Remedies
  • Prevention Tips
  • When to See a Dermatologist

What is Hormonal Acne?

Hormonal acne is acne that happens mainly because of changes in your body's hormones. While regular acne can affect teenagers, hormonal acne often affects adults, especially women.

  • • Main difference: Regular acne often improves with basic treatments, but hormonal acne keeps coming back
  • • Common age: Can start in teens but often continues into 20s, 30s, and even 40s
  • • Pattern: Often follows menstrual cycle in women

Common Signs of Hormonal Acne

How do you know if your acne is hormonal? Look for these signs:

  • • Location: Mostly on lower face - chin, jawline, and cheeks
  • • Type of pimples: Deep, painful bumps rather than surface blackheads
  • • Timing: Breakouts around periods (7-10 days before period starts)
  • • Other signs: May notice oilier skin during breakouts
  • • Response to treatment: Regular acne treatments may not work well

Main Causes of Hormonal Acne

1. Hormone Changes During Menstrual Cycle

In women, hormones change throughout the month. Before periods, progesterone levels rise, which can increase oil production and lead to breakouts.

2. Androgen Hormones

Androgens are male hormones that both men and women have. When these are too high, they can:

  • • Make skin produce more oil
  • • Clog pores more easily
  • • Cause inflammation

3. PCOS (Polycystic Ovary Syndrome)

Many women with PCOS have hormonal acne because of higher androgen levels. Other signs of PCOS include:

  • • Irregular periods
  • • Weight gain
  • • Excess facial hair

4. Stress

When you're stressed, your body makes more cortisol (stress hormone). This can trigger more oil production and breakouts.

5. Diet Factors

Certain foods may make hormonal acne worse for some people. Learn more about how diet affects skin health:

  • • High sugar foods
  • • Dairy products (in some people)
  • • Processed foods

6. Medications

Some medicines, like certain birth control pills or steroids, can affect hormones and cause acne.

Who Gets Hormonal Acne?

  • • Teenagers: During puberty when hormones change rapidly
  • • Women in 20s and 30s: Especially around menstrual cycles
  • • Pregnant women: Hormone changes during pregnancy
  • • Women approaching menopause: Hormone fluctuations
  • • People with PCOS or thyroid issues

Treatment Options for Hormonal Acne

1. Topical Treatments (Applied on Skin)

These are creams, gels, or lotions you apply directly on acne:

  • • Retinoids: Help unclog pores and reduce inflammation
  • • Benzoyl Peroxide: Kills bacteria and reduces oil
  • • Azelaic Acid: Reduces swelling and kills bacteria
  • • Salicylic Acid: Helps unclog pores

2. Oral Medications (Prescription)

For moderate to severe hormonal acne, your dermatologist may prescribe:

  • • Oral Contraceptives: Certain birth control pills help balance hormones
  • • Anti-Androgen Drugs: Reduce effects of male hormones
  • • Antibiotics: For short-term use to reduce inflammation
  • • Isotretinoin: For severe, resistant cases (requires careful monitoring)

3. Professional Treatments at Our Ajmer Clinic

At my dermatology clinic in Ajmer, we offer various skin treatments including:

  • • Chemical Peels: Help clear pores and reduce acne
  • • Laser Treatments: Reduce inflammation and prevent new breakouts
  • • Extraction Procedures: Safely remove deep pimples
  • • Cortisone Injections: For quick reduction of large, painful pimples

Helpful Home Remedies

Along with medical treatment, these home remedies from our guide on acne treatment at home can help:

1. Green Tea

  • • How it helps: Contains antioxidants that reduce inflammation
  • • How to use: Drink 2-3 cups daily or apply cooled green tea as toner

2. Tea Tree Oil

  • • How it helps: Natural antibacterial properties
  • • How to use: Mix 1-2 drops with 1 tablespoon carrier oil, apply on pimples
  • • Warning: Always dilute, never use pure on skin

3. Zinc Supplements

  • • How it helps: Reduces inflammation and oil production
  • • Food sources: Pumpkin seeds, chickpeas, lentils
  • • Supplement: Consult doctor before starting

4. Spearmint Tea

  • • How it helps: May help reduce androgen levels
  • • How to use: Drink 1-2 cups daily

Prevention Tips for Hormonal Acne

  1. Follow Consistent Skincare: Learn the perfect morning and night skincare routine for Indian skin
  2. Manage Stress: Practice yoga, meditation, or deep breathing exercises
  3. Healthy Diet: Follow tips from our guide on how diet affects skin health
  4. Don't Pick Pimples: This causes scars and spreads bacteria
  5. Change Pillowcases Regularly: At least once a week
  6. Stay Hydrated: Drink plenty of water throughout the day
  7. Track Your Cycle: Note when breakouts happen to anticipate and prepare

When to See a Dermatologist

You should book a consultation at our dermatology clinic in Ajmer if:

  • • Over-the-counter products don't work after 6-8 weeks
  • • Acne is painful, deep, or cystic
  • • Acne is leaving dark spots or scars that need scar reduction treatment
  • • You have other symptoms like irregular periods or excess hair growth
  • • Acne is affecting your confidence or mental health

My Final Advice as Your Dermatologist

Hormonal acne can be frustrating because it often comes back despite your best efforts. But remember:

  • • Be patient: Treatments take 4-8 weeks to show results
  • • Be consistent: Follow your treatment plan regularly
  • • Don't give up: If one treatment doesn't work, there are others to try
  • • Address the root cause: Sometimes we need to treat underlying hormone issues

If you're struggling with hormonal acne in Ajmer, I'm here to help. We can create a personalized treatment plan that addresses both your skin symptoms and any underlying hormonal factors. For severe cases, we may recommend advanced laser skin resurfacing treatments.

For acne marks that remain after treatment, check our guide on removing pimple marks naturally or consider professional pigmentation treatment at our clinic.

For more skincare tips, explore our complete blogs collection or learn about other skin rejuvenation treatments we offer.

To your clear, healthy skin,

Dr. Anju Mehra, MD
Dermatologist & Skin Specialist, Ajmer
